steps.title: Identify the center and radius of circle M
steps.content:
The equation $(x + 6a)^2 + (y - 38a)^2 = 36a^2$ is in standard form. Rewrite it as $(x - (-6a))^2 + (y - 38a)^2 = (6a)^2$ to identify:
  • Center M: $(-6a, 38a)$
  • Radius: $6a$

steps.title: Apply the rightward shift to find circle V
steps.content:
Shifting circle M rightward by $12a$ units means adding $12a$ to the x-coordinate of its center:
New center V: $(-6a + 12a, 38a) = (6a, 38a)$

The y-coordinate and radius remain unchanged, so circle V has equation:
$$(x - 6a)^2 + (y - 38a)^2 = 36a^2$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.
